questions.question: What is the function of the EFLG register in MCP2515?
questions.explanation: EFLG (Error Flag) register contains bits that indicate various error states like warning, passive, bus-off, and overflow conditions.

questions.question: What is the standard termination resistor value for CAN bus?
questions.explanation: CAN bus requires 120Ω termination resistors at each end of the bus to prevent signal reflections.

questions.question: In CAN bus arbitration, which message has higher priority?
questions.explanation: CAN uses dominant-first arbitration. Lower ID numbers have more dominant bits and win arbitration.

questions.question: What happens when a CAN node enters bus-off state?
options: It stops participating on the bus
questions.explanation: Bus-off state occurs after excessive errors. The node disconnects from the bus and must be reset to rejoin.

questions.question: For MCP2515 with 16MHz crystal, what gives approximately 500kbps?
